behaviors.description: The cron parser rejects invalid expressions (e.g., month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with a descriptive error message.
behaviors.justification: Cron parsing is deterministic, pure logic with no external dependencies. Invalid input validation and error message generation can be exhaustively tested by passing malformed strings and verifying exception types and descriptive messages. No system state, I/O, or mocking required.
behaviors.test_description: Test parser with invalid inputs including out-of-range values (month 13, day 32), malformed syntax (extra fields, non-numeric characters), and boundary violations. Verify each case raises a descriptive error naming the offending field and valid range. Include edge cases: month=12 and day=31 (valid), month=0 and day=0 (invalid).

behaviors.description: The next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard cron expressions, including edge cases like 'last day of month' and 'nearest weekday.'
behaviors.justification: Next-run-time computation is pure logic: given a cron expression and explicit start time, deterministically calculate subsequent run times. Edge cases (last day of month, Feb 29 leap years, nearest weekday) are calendar calculations without I/O or system state dependency. All outcomes are computable with fixed inputs.
behaviors.test_description: Test calculator with fixed start dates and standard expressions covering: standard intervals (daily, weekly, monthly), last-day-of-month cases (Feb 28/29 across leap/non-leap years), nearest weekday shifts, and combinations. Pass explicit start times (e.g., 2026-03-15 13:00) and verify computed next N times match known-correct expected values.

behaviors.description: The overlap detector identifies when two schedules would fire within the configured minimum gap (e.g., 5 minutes) and reports the conflicting time windows.
behaviors.justification: Overlap detection is pure logic comparing two schedule objects: check if computed run times fall within a threshold gap. No I/O, system state, or external dependency—only schedule comparison. All cases are testable by constructing test schedule objects and verifying the boolean/reporting logic.
behaviors.test_description: Test detector with pairs of schedules at various gaps relative to a 5-minute threshold. Verify: no overlap when gap >= 5 min, overlap detected when gap < 5 min, and reported conflict windows are correct. Test with schedules firing seconds apart, exactly at threshold, beyond threshold, and with non-overlapping schedules.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling produces correct run times across a DST transition—a job scheduled for 2:30 AM handles the spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity correctly.
behaviors.justification: Real DST failures depend on the system's actual timezone database (zoneinfo), which varies by OS and is periodically updated. Unit tests with mocked or bundled timezone data (e.g., pytz) may pass but hide production bugs when the system's database differs. Testing against actual system timezone data is the only reliable way to catch real DST boundary failures (non-existent times in spring-forward, ambiguous times in fall-back).
behaviors.test_description: Test scheduling across actual DST transitions using the system's real timezone database (zoneinfo). Schedule jobs for times that don't exist (spring-forward, e.g., 2:30 AM US/Eastern on 2026-03-08) and ambiguous times (fall-back, 2:30 AM on 2026-11-01). Verify correct handling: jump to valid post-gap time for non-existent times, and correct disambiguation for ambiguous times. Test with multiple timezones with different DST rules.

behaviors.description: When a scheduled job's computed next run time has already passed (e.g., system was down), the engine determines whether to skip or backfill based on the job's configured missed-run policy.
behaviors.justification: Missed-run policy application is conditional logic: if current_time > scheduled_time, apply the configured policy (skip vs. backfill). Time can be reliably mocked by controlling clock function returns. The logic does not require background threads, async coordination, or real timing races unless the scheduler has those complexities not mentioned in the issue.
behaviors.test_description: Test policy with mocked current time advanced past the scheduled run time. For each policy ('skip', 'backfill'), verify: skip policy does not queue missed runs, backfill policy queues all missed runs at the missed times. Test with various gaps: minutes past (5 min, 30 min), hours past (2 hrs, 24 hrs), days past. Verify no runs are duplicated or omitted.
